title: AI may not make us stupid, but relying on it could weaken the skills built through practice
excerpt: The worry of becoming stupid isn't new; it dates back to the time of the Greek philosopher Socrates, who insisted that writing would destroy memory and weaken the mind. Then came calculators, GPS and computers, all of which again raised concerns that we might forget basic mental skills and lose the battle against cognitive offloading. Cognitive offloading is the act of reducing mental effort by using external tools or aids to handle tasks that have historically relied solely on memory or internal processing. A similar sentiment is being observed with the advent of AI.
content_html:
The worry of becoming stupid isn't new; it dates back to the time of the Greek philosopher Socrates, who insisted that writing would destroy memory and weaken the mind. Then came calculators, GPS and computers, all of which again raised concerns that we might forget basic mental skills and lose the battle against cognitive offloading. Cognitive offloading is the act of reducing mental effort by using external tools or aids to handle tasks that have historically relied solely on memory or internal processing. A similar sentiment is being observed with the advent of AI.

A group of researchers set out to investigate an important question: Is AI making us stupid? To find the answer, they reviewed the existing scientific evidence on how using AI to perform mental tasks affects our brains and, particularly, our skills.

The answer is "yes" and "no." What they found was that the impact of AI depends largely on how we use it—whether we use it as a replacement for thinking or as a partner to accomplish our work.

For example, if someone asks an AI to solve a math problem instead of working through it themselves, they miss out on the practice needed to build that skill. Similarly, using AI to summarize information often leads people to put less effort into learning, so they tend to remember less of what they learn as AI is left to handle the hard work of searching, organizing and assessing information.

When using a tutor to fill in knowledge gaps for high school math students, they found that, instead of asking for an answer, their skills developed just as well as students with no AI access at all. The findings are published in Trends in Cognitive Sciences.

How you use it matters

A big concern is that using AI to think for us might make our own minds worse at those tasks when AI isn't around to help. There are two things that need to be considered: skills and basic cognitive abilities.

Skills are the things we learn to do. They combine relevant knowledge with practice, enabling us to perform tasks effectively. Basic mental abilities, however, are different. They are the brain's fundamental, general-purpose functions, such as working memory and attention, that provide the foundation for learning, development and the use of all our specific skills.

When it comes to skills, practice is what builds them. So when we offload that practice to AI, we may never develop the skill in the first place. Even skills we've already mastered begin to decay without regular practice. Practice is one of our strongest defenses against forgetting.

Where the bigger risks lie

There are also concerns about how long-term offloading might affect foundational brain functions over decades, as well as its impact on children during critical stages of development.

In this study, the researchers systematically reviewed existing research from cognitive science, education, medicine and human factors research. They drew on studies of math learning, online information-seeking and AI-assisted medical screening to understand how AI influences the way we think and learn. By focusing on skills, they were able to examine whether we are losing specific how-to knowledge while our basic brainpower remains intact.

Long-term effects remain unclear

The researchers found that these negative effects are possible, but not inevitable. At the same time, it's still too early to know what relying on AI will mean in the long run. Current evidence suggests that everything comes down to how we use AI. Used the right way, AI can boost learning, not by thinking for us, but by working alongside us as a collaborator and a source of feedback.

The real impact of AI on our skills and cognitive abilities won't be clear until long-term studies track how people use it across different tasks and professions.

Written for you by our author Sanjukta Mondal, edited by Sadie Harley, and fact-checked and reviewed by Robert Egan—this article is the result of careful human work. We rely on readers like you to keep independent science journalism alive. If this reporting matters to you, please consider a donation (especially monthly). You'll get an ad-free account as a thank-you.

Publication details

Trent N. Cash et al, Is AI making us stupid?, Trends in Cognitive Sciences (2026). DOI: 10.1016/j.tics.2026.06.004

Then came calculators, GPS and computers, all of which again raised concerns that we might forget basic mental skills and lose the battle against cognitive offloading. Cognitive offloading is the act of reducing mental effort by using external tools or aids to handle tasks that have historically relied solely on memory or internal processing. The findings are published in Trends in Cognitive Sciences. AI can potentially improve learning when used as a collaborator or source of worked examples. sentences.en: Completely handing cognitive work to AI can hinder learning because people lose the practice needed to build skills.
sentences.zh: 将认知工作完全交给人工智能可能会阻碍学习，因为人们失去了培养技能所需的实践。

sentences.en: Credit: Pexels.
sentences.zh: 图片来源：Pexels。

blocks.html_en: The worry of becoming stupid isn't new; it dates back to the time of the Greek philosopher Socrates, who insisted that writing would destroy memory and weaken the mind. Then came calculators, GPS and computers, all of which again raised concerns that we might forget basic mental skills and lose the battle against cognitive offloading. Cognitive offloading is the act of reducing mental effort by using external tools or aids to handle tasks that have historically relied solely on memory or internal processing. A similar sentiment is being observed with the advent of AI.

sentences.en: The worry of becoming stupid isn't new; it dates back to the time of the Greek philosopher Socrates, who insisted that writing would destroy memory and weaken the mind.
sentences.zh: 担心变得愚蠢并不新鲜。它可以追溯到希腊哲学家苏格拉底的时代，他坚持认为书写会破坏记忆并削弱思想。

sentences.en: Then came calculators, GPS and computers, all of which again raised concerns that we might forget basic mental skills and lose the battle against cognitive offloading.
sentences.zh: 随后出现了计算器、GPS 和计算机，所有这些再次引发了人们的担忧，即我们可能会忘记基本的心理技能并在与认知卸载的战斗中失败。

sentences.en: Cognitive offloading is the act of reducing mental effort by using external tools or aids to handle tasks that have historically relied solely on memory or internal processing.
sentences.zh: 认知卸载是通过使用外部工具或辅助工具来处理历史上仅依赖于记忆或内部处理的任务来减少脑力劳动的行为。

sentences.en: A similar sentiment is being observed with the advent of AI.
sentences.zh: 随着人工智能的出现，人们也观察到了类似的情绪。

blocks.html_en: A group of researchers set out to investigate an important question: Is AI making us stupid? To find the answer, they reviewed the existing scientific evidence on how using AI to perform mental tasks affects our brains and, particularly, our skills.

sentences.en: A group of researchers set out to investigate an important question: Is AI making us stupid?
sentences.zh: 一组研究人员着手调查一个重要问题：人工智能是否让我们变得愚蠢？

sentences.en: To find the answer, they reviewed the existing scientific evidence on how using AI to perform mental tasks affects our brains and, particularly, our skills.
sentences.zh: 为了找到答案，他们回顾了现有的科学证据，证明使用人工智能执行心理任务如何影响我们的大脑，特别是我们的技能。

blocks.html_en: The answer is "yes" and "no." What they found was that the impact of AI depends largely on how we use it—whether we use it as a replacement for thinking or as a partner to accomplish our work.

sentences.en: The answer is "yes" and "no."
sentences.zh: 答案是“是”和“不是”。

sentences.en: What they found was that the impact of AI depends largely on how we use it—whether we use it as a replacement for thinking or as a partner to accomplish our work.
sentences.zh: 他们发现，人工智能的影响很大程度上取决于我们如何使用它——我们是用它来代替思考，还是作为完成工作的合作伙伴。

blocks.html_en: For example, if someone asks an AI to solve a math problem instead of working through it themselves, they miss out on the practice needed to build that skill. Similarly, using AI to summarize information often leads people to put less effort into learning, so they tend to remember less of what they learn as AI is left to handle the hard work of searching, organizing and assessing information.

sentences.en: For example, if someone asks an AI to solve a math problem instead of working through it themselves, they miss out on the practice needed to build that skill.
sentences.zh: 例如，如果有人要求人工智能解决数学问题而不是自己解决数学问题，他们就会错过培养该技能所需的实践。

sentences.en: Similarly, using AI to summarize information often leads people to put less effort into learning, so they tend to remember less of what they learn as AI is left to handle the hard work of searching, organizing and assessing information.
sentences.zh: 同样，使用人工智能来总结信息通常会导致人们在学习上投入更少的精力，因此他们往往会记住更少的知识，因为人工智能会处理搜索、组织和评估信息的艰苦工作。

blocks.html_en: When using a tutor to fill in knowledge gaps for high school math students, they found that, instead of asking for an answer, their skills developed just as well as students with no AI access at all. The findings are published in Trends in Cognitive Sciences.

sentences.en: When using a tutor to fill in knowledge gaps for high school math students, they found that, instead of asking for an answer, their skills developed just as well as students with no AI access at all.
sentences.zh: 当使用导师来填补高中数学学生的知识空白时，他们发现，他们的技能发展得和根本没有人工智能访问权限的学生一样好，而不是寻求答案。

sentences.en: The findings are published in Trends in Cognitive Sciences.
sentences.zh: 研究结果发表在《认知科学趋势》上。

sentences.en: AI can potentially improve learning when used as a collaborator or source of worked examples.
sentences.zh: 当人工智能用作协作者或工作示例来源时，可以潜在地改善学习。

sentences.en: Credit: www.kaboompics.com/Pexels
sentences.zh: 图片来源：www.kaboompics.com/Pexels

blocks.html_en: How you use it matters

sentences.en: How you use it matters
sentences.zh: 你如何使用它很重要

blocks.html_en: A big concern is that using AI to think for us might make our own minds worse at those tasks when AI isn't around to help. There are two things that need to be considered: skills and basic cognitive abilities.

sentences.en: A big concern is that using AI to think for us might make our own minds worse at those tasks when AI isn't around to help.
sentences.zh: 一个大问题是，当人工智能无法提供帮助时，使用人工智能为我们思考可能会让我们自己的思维在这些任务上变得更糟。

sentences.en: There are two things that need to be considered: skills and basic cognitive abilities.
sentences.zh: 有两件事需要考虑：技能和基本认知能力。

blocks.html_en: Skills are the things we learn to do. They combine relevant knowledge with practice, enabling us to perform tasks effectively. Basic mental abilities, however, are different. They are the brain's fundamental, general-purpose functions, such as working memory and attention, that provide the foundation for learning, development and the use of all our specific skills.

sentences.en: Skills are the things we learn to do.
sentences.zh: 技能是我们学会做的事情。

sentences.en: They combine relevant knowledge with practice, enabling us to perform tasks effectively.
sentences.zh: 他们将相关知识与实践相结合，使我们能够有效地执行任务。

sentences.en: Basic mental abilities, however, are different.
sentences.zh: 然而，基本的心理能力是不同的。

sentences.en: They are the brain's fundamental, general-purpose functions, such as working memory and attention, that provide the foundation for learning, development and the use of all our specific skills.
sentences.zh: 它们是大脑的基本通用功能，例如工作记忆和注意力，为学习、发展和使用我们所有特定技能提供了基础。

blocks.html_en: When it comes to skills, practice is what builds them. So when we offload that practice to AI, we may never develop the skill in the first place. Even skills we've already mastered begin to decay without regular practice. Practice is one of our strongest defenses against forgetting.

sentences.en: When it comes to skills, practice is what builds them.
sentences.zh: 就技能而言，实践是建立技能的基础。

sentences.en: So when we offload that practice to AI, we may never develop the skill in the first place.
sentences.zh: 因此，当我们将这种实践转移给人工智能时，我们可能永远不会首先发展这项技能。

sentences.en: Even skills we've already mastered begin to decay without regular practice.
sentences.zh: 如果不定期练习，即使我们已经掌握的技能也会开始衰退。

sentences.en: Practice is one of our strongest defenses against forgetting.
sentences.zh: 练习是我们防止遗忘的最有力的防御措施之一。

blocks.html_en: Where the bigger risks lie

sentences.en: Where the bigger risks lie
sentences.zh: 更大的风险在哪里

blocks.html_en: There are also concerns about how long-term offloading might affect foundational brain functions over decades, as well as its impact on children during critical stages of development.

sentences.en: There are also concerns about how long-term offloading might affect foundational brain functions over decades, as well as its impact on children during critical stages of development.
sentences.zh: 人们还担心几十年来长期的减负可能会如何影响大脑的基本功能，以及它对处于发育关键阶段的儿童的影响。

blocks.html_en: In this study, the researchers systematically reviewed existing research from cognitive science, education, medicine and human factors research. They drew on studies of math learning, online information-seeking and AI-assisted medical screening to understand how AI influences the way we think and learn. By focusing on skills, they were able to examine whether we are losing specific how-to knowledge while our basic brainpower remains intact.

sentences.en: In this study, the researchers systematically reviewed existing research from cognitive science, education, medicine and human factors research.
sentences.zh: 在这项研究中，研究人员系统地回顾了认知科学、教育、医学和人为因素研究的现有研究。

sentences.en: They drew on studies of math learning, online information-seeking and AI-assisted medical screening to understand how AI influences the way we think and learn.
sentences.zh: 他们利用数学学习、在线信息查找和人工智能辅助医疗筛查的研究来了解人工智能如何影响我们的思维和学习方式。

sentences.en: By focusing on skills, they were able to examine whether we are losing specific how-to knowledge while our basic brainpower remains intact.
sentences.zh: 通过关注技能，他们能够检查我们是否正在失去具体的操作知识，而我们的基本脑力仍然完好无损。

blocks.html_en: Long-term effects remain unclear

sentences.en: Long-term effects remain unclear
sentences.zh: 长期影响仍不清楚

blocks.html_en: The researchers found that these negative effects are possible, but not inevitable. At the same time, it's still too early to know what relying on AI will mean in the long run. Current evidence suggests that everything comes down to how we use AI. Used the right way, AI can boost learning, not by thinking for us, but by working alongside us as a collaborator and a source of feedback.

sentences.en: The researchers found that these negative effects are possible, but not inevitable.
sentences.zh: 研究人员发现这些负面影响是可能的，但并非不可避免。

sentences.en: At the same time, it's still too early to know what relying on AI will mean in the long run.
sentences.zh: 与此同时，从长远来看，判断依赖人工智能意味着什么还为时过早。

sentences.en: Current evidence suggests that everything comes down to how we use AI.
sentences.zh: 目前的证据表明，一切都取决于我们如何使用人工智能。

sentences.en: Used the right way, AI can boost learning, not by thinking for us, but by working alongside us as a collaborator and a source of feedback.
sentences.zh: 如果使用得当，人工智能可以促进学习，不是通过为我们思考，而是通过作为合作者和反馈来源与我们一起工作。

blocks.html_en: The real impact of AI on our skills and cognitive abilities won't be clear until long-term studies track how people use it across different tasks and professions.

sentences.en: The real impact of AI on our skills and cognitive abilities won't be clear until long-term studies track how people use it across different tasks and professions.
sentences.zh: 人工智能对我们的技能和认知能力的真正影响只有在长期研究跟踪人们如何在不同的任务和职业中使用它时才会清楚。

sentences.en: The content is provided for information purposes only.
sentences.zh: 所提供的内容仅供参考。

content_html_zh:
担心变得愚蠢并不新鲜。它可以追溯到希腊哲学家苏格拉底的时代，他坚持认为书写会破坏记忆并削弱思想。 随后出现了计算器、GPS 和计算机，所有这些再次引发了人们的担忧，即我们可能会忘记基本的心理技能并在与认知卸载的战斗中失败。 认知卸载是通过使用外部工具或辅助工具来处理历史上仅依赖于记忆或内部处理的任务来减少脑力劳动的行为。 随着人工智能的出现，人们也观察到了类似的情绪。

一组研究人员着手调查一个重要问题：人工智能是否让我们变得愚蠢？ 为了找到答案，他们回顾了现有的科学证据，证明使用人工智能执行心理任务如何影响我们的大脑，特别是我们的技能。

答案是“是”和“不是”。 他们发现，人工智能的影响很大程度上取决于我们如何使用它——我们是用它来代替思考，还是作为完成工作的合作伙伴。

例如，如果有人要求人工智能解决数学问题而不是自己解决数学问题，他们就会错过培养该技能所需的实践。 同样，使用人工智能来总结信息通常会导致人们在学习上投入更少的精力，因此他们往往会记住更少的知识，因为人工智能会处理搜索、组织和评估信息的艰苦工作。

当使用导师来填补高中数学学生的知识空白时，他们发现，他们的技能发展得和根本没有人工智能访问权限的学生一样好，而不是寻求答案。 研究结果发表在《认知科学趋势》上。

你如何使用它很重要

一个大问题是，当人工智能无法提供帮助时，使用人工智能为我们思考可能会让我们自己的思维在这些任务上变得更糟。 有两件事需要考虑：技能和基本认知能力。

技能是我们学会做的事情。 他们将相关知识与实践相结合，使我们能够有效地执行任务。 然而，基本的心理能力是不同的。 它们是大脑的基本通用功能，例如工作记忆和注意力，为学习、发展和使用我们所有特定技能提供了基础。

就技能而言，实践是建立技能的基础。 因此，当我们将这种实践转移给人工智能时，我们可能永远不会首先发展这项技能。 如果不定期练习，即使我们已经掌握的技能也会开始衰退。 练习是我们防止遗忘的最有力的防御措施之一。

更大的风险在哪里

人们还担心几十年来长期的减负可能会如何影响大脑的基本功能，以及它对处于发育关键阶段的儿童的影响。

在这项研究中，研究人员系统地回顾了认知科学、教育、医学和人为因素研究的现有研究。 他们利用数学学习、在线信息查找和人工智能辅助医疗筛查的研究来了解人工智能如何影响我们的思维和学习方式。 通过关注技能，他们能够检查我们是否正在失去具体的操作知识，而我们的基本脑力仍然完好无损。

长期影响仍不清楚

研究人员发现这些负面影响是可能的，但并非不可避免。 与此同时，从长远来看，判断依赖人工智能意味着什么还为时过早。 目前的证据表明，一切都取决于我们如何使用人工智能。 如果使用得当，人工智能可以促进学习，不是通过为我们思考，而是通过作为合作者和反馈来源与我们一起工作。

人工智能对我们的技能和认知能力的真正影响只有在长期研究跟踪人们如何在不同的任务和职业中使用它时才会清楚。

本文由我们的作者 Sanjukta Mondal 为您撰写，由 Sadie Harley 编辑，并由 Robert Egan 进行事实检查和审阅——本文是人类精心工作的结果。 我们依靠像您这样的读者来保持独立科学新闻的活力。 如果此报告对您很重要，请考虑捐款（尤其是每月）。 作为感谢，您将获得一个无广告帐户。

特伦特·N. Cash 等人，人工智能让我们变得愚蠢吗？，认知科学趋势（2026）。 DOI：10.1016/j.tics.2026.06.004

桑朱克塔·蒙达尔

萨迪·哈雷

罗伯特·伊根
